description: The Monitors class describes the set of all installed monitor objects. It can be accessed with the monitors object, found in the automatically imported td module. It operates much like a Python list of monitor objects.

members.name: width
members.description: The width of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

members.name: height
members.description: The height of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

members.name: left
members.description: The leftmost edge of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

members.name: right
members.description: The rightmost edge of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

members.name: top
members.description: The topmost position of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

members.name: bottom
members.description: The bottommost position of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

methods.name: locate
methods.description: Return the monitor at the specified mouse coordinates, or None.

methods.name: refresh
methods.description: Causes the application to behave as if a monitor device has changed. Monitors DAT and other sources will be updated. This is typically done automatically by the operating system, but in special cases can be triggered manually with this method.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the Monitors Class wiki page
